name: Adour and Chalosse Circuit
name_original: De l'Adour à la Midouze — boucle au départ de Dax
description: This moderate 47.1-mile touring cycling loop from Dax explores the Adour and Midouze rivers, offering diverse landscapes and cultural sites.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Dax offers routes through diverse landscapes in the Landes region of southwestern France. The area features extensive pine forests, numerous lakes, and riverbanks along the Adour and Midouze. Its generally flat topography makes it suitable for various cycling abilities, with some routes extending towards the Atlantic coast. Best touring cycling routes around Dax The most popular touring cycling route is Between pines, lakes and ocean — Les Landes, a 17.6 miles (28.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This route navigates through tranquil pine forests and past scenic lakes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is The trails under the pines — loop from Léon, a moderate 15.1 miles (24.4 km) path. This route offers shaded cycling through the region's characteristic pine forests. Local touring cyclists also love the Landes Forest – former train station loop from Moliets-et-Maâ, a 25.9 miles (41.7 km) trail leading through the vast Landes Forest, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Dax is defined by extensive pine forests, serene lakes, and picturesque riverbanks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy, flat paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Dax are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 7,000 reviews. More than 51,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Dax's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Dax?
A: The Dax region offers a vast network for touring cyclists, with over 1,100 routes available. These range from easy, flat paths to more challenging rides, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What is the general terrain like for touring cycling in the Dax region?
A: The Landes region, where Dax is situated, is characterized by generally flat topography, making it ideal for touring cyclists who prefer less strenuous rides. You'll find many routes on mostly paved surfaces, winding through tranquil pine forests and along picturesque riverbanks.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easy ride?
A: Yes, Dax is very accommodating for beginners and those looking for an easy ride. Nearly 700 of the available routes are classified as easy, taking advantage of the region's flat terrain. An excellent option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1518081570/between-pines-lakes-and-ocean-les-landes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between pines, lakes and ocean — Les Landes&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 17.6-mile (28.3 km) trail.

Q: Are there more challenging touring cycling routes around Dax?
A: While much of the region is flat, there are routes that offer more elevation and challenge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1286765908/coteaux-et-vallons-de-chalosse-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coteaux et vallons de Chalosse — loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 42.1-mile (67.7 km) route with significant ascents, providing a good workout for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Dax?
A: Touring cycling around Dax offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll primarily encounter vast, tranquil pine forests, picturesque lakes like L'Etang Blanc, and scenic riverbanks along the Adour and Midouze rivers. Some routes also offer proximity to the Atlantic coast, providing glimpses of superb Landes beaches.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Many touring cycling routes around Dax are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284217107/the-trails-under-the-pines-loop-from-leon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The trails under the pines — loop from Léon&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284200788/around-the-etang-blanc-loop-from-seignosse" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Around the Etang blanc — loop from Seignosse&lt;/a&gt;, which offer convenient circular journeys through varied scenery.

Q: Can I cycle along the coast or near the ocean from Dax?
A: While Dax is inland, its proximity to the Atlantic coast means you can easily access coastal routes. The Vélodyssée, a major cycle route, runs along the coast, and routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40071788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hossegor Lake – Port of Capbreton loop from Moliets-et-Maâ&lt;/a&gt; allow you to discover superb Landes beaches and the magnificent Hossegor marine lake.

Q: What natural attractions can I discover along touring cycling routes near Dax?
A: The region boasts several natural highlights accessible by bike. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Courant d'Huchet Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, cycle along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3551855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Léon Lake Trail&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3467910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;West Lake of Soustons&lt;/a&gt;. Dax is also famous for its thermal springs, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2918452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Hot Fountain&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical landmarks to visit while cycling in Dax?
A: Dax itself is rich in history and culture. After your ride, you can explore the historic center with its Roman heritage, visit the Basilique Gallo-romaine de Dax, or relax at the famous Place de la Fontaine Chaude. The town also features green spaces like Parc du Sarrat and Parc des Arènes de Dax, blending history with nature.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Dax?
A: The Landes region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for longer tours. Summer can be warm, but many routes, especially those through the extensive pine forests, offer welcome shade. Winter cycling is possible, though cooler temperatures and occasional rain should be expected.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes around Dax?
A: Yes, the generally flat topography and extensive network of protected cycle paths make Dax an excellent destination for family cycling. Many routes are easy and mostly paved, suitable for all ages. The shaded paths through pine forests are particularly pleasant for families.

Q: Where can I find parking for my bike tour in Dax?
A: Dax offers various parking options for cyclists. You can typically find parking near the train station or in the city center. Many trailheads for popular routes also have designated parking areas, making it convenient to start your tour.

Q: Is it possible to access touring cycling routes using public transport in Dax?
A: Public transport options in Dax can help you access various starting points for touring cycling routes. Local bus services often accommodate bicycles, especially outside of peak hours. It's advisable to check with the specific transport provider for their current bike policies and schedules.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Dax?
A: The touring cycling experience around Dax is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7,000 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the extensive network of well-maintained paths, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the picturesque scenery along lakes and rivers, making it a popular destination for all levels.
tips: The Roman-style Cathedral was built in 1656. It has been listed as a historic monument since 1946.
Do you smell this smell? It is the thermal water that springs naturally in Dax. If you approach, have fun touching the water that comes out of the fountain, you can see that it is naturally hot.
The Sacred Forest is an imposing oak grove, classified in 1941 and whose age of certain oaks reaches or exceeds 300 years.

faqs.question: Where does the 'From the Adour to the Midouze — loop from Dax' cycling route start, and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Dax. As Dax is a well-known spa town, you'll find various parking options within the town to begin your cycling adventure.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bicycles. You'll also encounter some compacted earth sections. Expect normal undulations throughout the ride, with a notable 'rather steep climb' bringing you to Poyanne.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: This loop offers diverse scenery, including areas of crops, meadows, and wooded stretches. A significant feature is the presence of 'barthes'—wetland meadows along the Adour River—which are protected Natura 2000 sites. Keep an eye out for various birds like herons and storks, and you might even spot semi-wild horses. You'll also enjoy views of the Adour and Midouze rivers, charming villages, and panoramas towards the Pyrenees mountains.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, cycling in the Landes department is generally pleasant during spring and early autumn when the weather is mild and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be warm, but the route's varied terrain and river proximity might offer some respite.

faqs.question: Are there any notable cultural or natural landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting spots. In Dax, you can see the iconic The Hot Fountain. The loop also takes you through areas with rich religious heritage, such as Carcarès-Sainte-Croix with its fortified church, and Saint-Vincent-de-Paul, where you can visit the birthplace of the saint. Buglose is another significant pilgrimage site known for its basilica. You'll also pass Le Bois Sacré, a forest area.

faqs.question: Is the 'From the Adour to the Midouze — loop from Dax' suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and requires 'good fitness' due to its distance and elevation gain. While surfaces are mostly paved, the length (75.8 km) and some undulations, including a steep climb to Poyanne, mean it's best suited for cyclists with some experience and a reasonable fitness level rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other cycling routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is part of a broader network of cycling routes in the Landes department. It overlaps with sections of well-known paths such as the EV3 France 46, Mont-de-Marsan - Dax (50% of the route), and the Voie verte de Chalosse : Liaison Dax (5%). You'll also find smaller sections of routes like the Circuit entre Adour et Chalosse and the Circuit VTT des chemins de halage de l'Adour et de la Midouze.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The information available does not specify whether dogs are permitted on this particular cycling route. Given it's a touring bicycle route, dogs would typically need to be carried in a suitable bike trailer or basket if allowed. Always check local regulations for specific sections, especially in protected natural areas like the 'barthes'.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public route. You can enjoy the natural and cultural landscapes freely.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or water along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop passes through several villages and towns, including Dax, which is a major hub. You should find opportunities for refreshments and water refills in these populated areas. However, it's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this touring bicycle trip?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring bicycle route, bring a well-maintained touring bicycle, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. Given the varied scenery, a camera might also be a good idea. Sun protection is recommended, especially during warmer months.
